report_name: Microsoft's May 2026 Patch Tuesday: Over 130 Flaws Fixed, Including Critical RCEs

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-41089
vulnerabilities.description: A critical stack-based buffer overflow in the Windows Netlogon service. An unauthenticated attacker on the same network could send a specially crafted request to a domain controller to achieve remote code execution with SYSTEM privileges.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-41096
vulnerabilities.description: A critical heap-based buffer overflow in the Windows DNS Client. This flaw can be triggered when a client receives a malicious response from an attacker-controlled DNS server, leading to remote code execution on the client machine.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-41103
vulnerabilities.description: A critical elevation of privilege vulnerability in the Microsoft SSO Plugin for Jira & Confluence. This flaw allows an attacker to bypass authentication by forging a credential response, granting unauthorized access.
affected_products: Microsoft SSO Plugin for Jira & Confluence

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-40361
vulnerabilities.description: A use-after-free vulnerability in Microsoft Word. Exploitation occurs when a user opens or previews a malicious document, which could lead to remote code execution in the context of the user.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-35421
vulnerabilities.description: A heap-based buffer overflow in the Windows Graphics Device Interface (GDI). This can be exploited by tricking a user into opening a specially crafted Enhanced Metafile (EMF) image.

indicators.name: LSASS Process Crash on Domain Controller
indicators.pattern:
title: LSASS Process Crash on Domain Controller
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a90-8bcd-ef1234567890
status: experimental
description: Detects a crash in the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (lsass.exe), which could indicate an attempt to exploit vulnerabilities like the Netlogon RCE CVE-2026-41089.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-may-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-120-flaws-no-zero-days/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-14
tags:
  - attack.t1068
level: high
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: application
detection:
  selection:
    Provider_Name: 'Application Error'
    EventID: 1000
    Data|contains: 'lsass.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects crashes of the lsass.exe process on a Domain Controller, which could indicate an exploitation attempt against CVE-2026-41089.

indicators.name: Suspicious Encoded PowerShell from Microsoft Office
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Encoded PowerShell from Microsoft Office
id: b2c3d4e5-f6a7-4891-8cde-f12345678901
status: experimental
description: Detects Microsoft Office applications spawning an encoded PowerShell command, a common post-exploitation technique for macro-based or vulnerability-driven attacks like those involving CVE-2026-40361.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-may-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-120-flaws-no-zero-days/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-14
tags:
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1059.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\WINWORD.EXE'
      - '\EXCEL.EXE'
      - '\POWERPNT.EXE'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -e '
      - ' -encodedcommand '
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects Microsoft Office applications (WINWORD.EXE, etc.) spawning an encoded PowerShell command, a hunting hint for post-exploitation following a successful exploit of vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-40361.

indicators.name: Windows DNS Client Malformed Response Error
indicators.pattern:
title: Windows DNS Client Malformed Response Error
id: c3d4e5f6-a7b8-4012-8def-123456789012
status: experimental
description: Detects Windows DNS Client event logs that may indicate a malformed response, potentially related to exploitation attempts against vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-41096.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-may-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-120-flaws-no-zero-days/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-14
tags:
  - attack.t1203
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: Microsoft-Windows-DNS-Client/Operational
detection:
  selection:
    EventID:
      - 8015
      - 8016
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects Windows DNS Client event logs (Event IDs 8015, 8016) that may indicate a malformed response, a potential indicator of exploitation attempts against CVE-2026-41096.

relationships.source_name: LSASS Process Crash on Domain Controller
Suspicious Encoded PowerShell from Microsoft Office
Windows DNS Client Malformed Response Error
